raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I purchased a XXXX XXXX for {$790.00} for laser hair removal services. I paid using my credit card through XXXX website. The service was advertised as XXXX sessions for a large area, with no exclusions listed. On XX/XX/XXXX, I attempted to redeem the voucher, but the merchant ( XXXX Aesthetics XXXX ) refused to provide the service, claiming the area was not included. They tried to upsell me and then blocked further communication. I never received any portion of the service. 

I contacted XXXX and submitted documentation showing that the voucher was marked redeemed even though no service was provided. XXXX refused to refund my payment and only offered {$150.00} in XXXX credit, which is not an acceptable resolution for a {$790.00} purchase. 

I filed a chargeback request with my credit card issuer, but it was denied. I was told the company could not assist because the transaction involved a third party. However, XXXX was the merchant of record and collected payment directly. This is a clear case of services not received, and I believe the chargeback should have been honored. 

I am requesting that the credit card issuer reconsider the chargeback and issue a full refund of {$790.00}, and that this case be reviewed as a failure to follow proper dispute resolution procedures. 

Barclaycard has refused to reverse the charge and stated that I am responsible for the amount. They claimed they investigated, but their explanation was vague and generic.
